page: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 32 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration allocates 32 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio on a shared CPU model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 640 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark evaluations demonstrate strong capabilities across compute and memory subsystems. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance reaches the top tier with a score of 3103.67, while multi-core performance registers in the top 25% at 99997.49. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 537.96 GB/sec and write speeds at 292.8 GB/sec, both placing in the top 10% tier. The system also demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 209.38 GB/sec and a PassMark CPU score of 63947.14, indicating robust parallel processing capabilities.

From a resource density perspective, this instance offers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-effective option for workloads requiring substantial memory and parallel processing without the premium of dedicated CPU pinning. However, the shared CPU allocation model represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, jitter-free maximum compute performance. Given its strong Redis SET throughput of 73.66 million operations per second and solid database operations benchmarks, this server is well-suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded data processing, caching layers, and medium-scale database deployments.
description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Milan processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 640 GB NVMe SSD. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model, this x86_64 server delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and exceptional large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. While the shared CPU model presents a tradeoff for workloads requiring guaranteed, non-shared compute resources, the instance provides excellent resource density for parallel processing. It is highly suited for general-purpose workloads, caching layers such as Redis, multi-threaded data processing, and database operations.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 640 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an AMD EPYC Milan server with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 640 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose and database workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, high-speed NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC Milan vCPUs with 128 GB of system memory
640 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier memory bandwidth and single-core CPU performance
Shared CPU allocation model with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Optimized for database operations, Redis caching, and general-purpose applications
